data.title: New Silkroad Culturaltainment warns of wider 2025 loss on impairment charges
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

New Silkroad Culturaltainment Ltd. ( (HK:0472) ) has shared an update.

New Silkroad Culturaltainment Limited has issued a profit warning, advising shareholders and potential investors that it expects a larger net loss attributable to owners for the year ended 31 December 2025, projected between HK$310 million and HK$350 million versus a HK$228 million loss a year earlier. The increased loss mainly stems from non-cash impairments of goodwill and property, plant and equipment, which management says should not affect operating cash flows or ongoing business operations.

The figures are based on unaudited consolidated management accounts and other internal information still subject to review by the audit committee and external auditors, with final annual results slated for release on 13 March 2026. The company cautioned investors to exercise care when trading its shares ahead of the formal results, underlining the potential for adjustments and the heightened earnings volatility implied by the sizeable impairment charges.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0472) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on New Silkroad Culturaltainment Ltd. stock, see the HK:0472 Stock Forecast page.

More about New Silkroad Culturaltainment Ltd.

New Silkroad Culturaltainment Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates through a group structure, though this announcement does not detail its specific business lines, products or geographic markets. The company is overseen by a board comprising five executive directors and three independent non-executive directors, reflecting a conventional governance structure for a mid-sized listed group.

Average Trading Volume: 1,427,767

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

An update from PATEO CONNECT Technology (Shanghai) Corporation Class H ( (HK:2889) ) is now available.

PATEO CONNECT Technology (Shanghai) Corporation has called an extraordinary general meeting for March 26, 2026, in Shanghai to seek shareholder approval for new share capital authorities. The board is asking investors to approve a general mandate to repurchase shares, as well as a mandate to issue new shares and sell or transfer treasury shares, which would give management greater flexibility in managing the company’s capital structure and potential future financing activities.

The company also detailed procedural arrangements for the meeting, including closure of the H-share register from March 23 to March 26, 2026, to determine eligibility to attend and vote. Shareholders may appoint one or more proxies, with specific documentation and submission deadlines set out for both domestic and H-share holders, underlining the formal corporate governance framework surrounding these proposed capital management measures.

The company has both H shares and domestic shares and is subject to Hong Kong listing and corporate governance requirements for shareholder meetings and voting procedures.

Star Plus Legend Holdings Limited ( (HK:6683) ) just unveiled an update.

Star Plus Legend Holdings Limited has warned that it expects to swing to a consolidated loss of up to RMB52 million for 2025, compared with a profit of RMB50.2 million a year earlier, as it slows IP content launches and absorbs margin pressure in its expanding new retail business. The company also booked non-cash impairment provisions on certain trade receivables amid geopolitical uncertainty, but said its cash flow remains solid.

Management described 2025 as a critical transition year marked by continued investment in upgrading its business structure, enhancing its IP ecosystem, and testing AI and robotics applications in celebrity IP scenarios. The board stressed that operations remain normal and framed the spending as strategic, high-barrier asset allocation intended to strengthen long-term growth prospects, while cautioning investors that final audited results may differ from preliminary figures.

data.title: Kingland Group Announces HK$152.9 Million Non-Underwritten Rights Issue
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Kingland Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:1751) ) is now available.

Kingland Group Holdings plans a non-underwritten rights issue, offering one rights share for every three shares held at a subscription price of HK$1.58, to raise up to about HK$152.9 million in gross proceeds, with estimated net proceeds of approximately HK$148.1 million. The offer is limited to qualifying shareholders on the record date, with no excess applications allowed and any unsubscribed or non-qualifying shareholders’ unsold rights shares to be placed on a best-effort basis to independent placees, potentially reducing the final size of the issue.

The company has entered into a placing agreement with an independent placing agent to implement compensatory arrangements required by Hong Kong listing rules, ensuring that any unsubscribed entitlements can be monetised for the benefit of the relevant shareholders. As the transaction falls within the thresholds of the Listing Rules, it does not require separate shareholder approval and is structured to comply with dilution limits, signalling a capital-raising move that aims to strengthen the balance sheet without breaching regulatory constraints or significantly diluting existing investors beyond permitted levels.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1751) stock is a Hold with a HK$2.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Kingland Group Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:1751 Stock Forecast page.

More about Kingland Group Holdings Limited

Tsaker New Energy Tech Co., Limited ( (HK:1986) ) just unveiled an update.

Tsaker New Energy Tech announced that several preferential investors in its Shandong TNM subsidiary, including Xinchuang Future, Anyida and Xingaodi Capital, have formally agreed not to exercise their contractual repurchase rights before 31 December 2029. These rights would have allowed them to require Shandong TNM and covenantors to buy back their equity if the unit failed to complete a qualified IPO by the end of 2026.

The investors’ decision, based on an assessment of the recovering new energy battery materials market, signals confidence in Shandong TNM’s future development and gives the subsidiary a longer runway to grow without the near‑term pressure of a potential equity repurchase. Tsaker New Energy Tech indicated it will update the market on any further changes to the repurchase arrangements in line with Hong Kong listing requirements.

Jiangsu Guofu Hydrogen Energy Equipment said its indirect wholly owned unit Xinjiang Guofu Mingzhi Hydrogen Energy Technology has signed a sales agreement with Hefei Zhongke Hecheng Green Energy for hydrogen production equipment. data.title: Jakota Capital Unveils Share Consolidation and Capital Reorganisation Plan
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Kingkey Financial International (Holdings) Ltd. ( (HK:1468) ) has shared an update.

Jakota Capital (Holding) Group plans a capital reorganisation that includes consolidating every five existing issued and unissued shares of HK$0.10 par value into one consolidated share of HK$0.50. The move is designed to restructure the share capital while keeping the trading board lot size unchanged at 10,000 shares, maintaining continuity for market participants.

Following the consolidation, the company will reduce the paid-up capital on each consolidated share by HK$0.40, lowering its par value from HK$0.50 to HK$0.10, and then subdivide each authorised but unissued consolidated share into five new shares of HK$0.10. These steps, which require shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting and are subject to conditions, aim to realign the company’s capital structure without disrupting trading arrangements, potentially improving capital flexibility and market perception.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1468) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.08 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Kingkey Financial International (Holdings) Ltd. stock, see the HK:1468 Stock Forecast page.

More about Kingkey Financial International (Holdings) Ltd.

Planetree International Development Limited has signalled a sharp turnaround in its financial performance for the year ended 31 December 2025, projecting a profit before tax of about HK$30 million versus a loss of roughly HK$212 million a year earlier. The improvement highlights the group’s sensitivity to market valuations and marks a notable recovery in its investment-driven business.

The expected profit is mainly driven by a net unrealized gain of approximately HK$150 million on financial assets, a HK$37 million reversal of impairment on margin loan receivables and around HK$48 million in profit contributions from associates. These gains are partly offset by a net realized loss of about HK$81 million on disposals of financial assets and a HK$94 million fair value loss on investment properties, while other comprehensive income is also set to swing positively, suggesting a stronger balance sheet position ahead of the final audited results.

data.description:

Seazen Group Ltd. ( (HK:1030) ) has shared an update.

Seazen Group Limited has announced the results of an offer by its subsidiary New Metro Global Limited to purchase for cash its outstanding 4.5% guaranteed senior notes due May 2026. The move concerns notes issued to international investors and is structured as an offshore transaction, with Seazen emphasizing that the offer is not a public securities offering in Hong Kong, the United States or other restricted jurisdictions.

The company stresses that the announcement is being posted in Hong Kong solely to ensure equal information dissemination and compliance with local listing rules, and it cautions that no investment decisions should be based solely on this notice. While detailed financial terms are not disclosed in the text provided, the partial repurchase of upcoming-maturity notes suggests Seazen is actively managing its debt profile, a step that may help address refinancing risk and signal commitment to meeting offshore obligations amid a challenging funding environment for Chinese issuers.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1030) stock is a Hold with a HK$2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Seazen Group Ltd. stock, see the HK:1030 Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Rongta Technology Warns of Sharp Profit Drop on Delays, Higher Costs
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9881) ) just unveiled an update.

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. has issued a profit warning, saying it expects to report net profit of at least RMB3.0 million for 2025, sharply lower than the RMB41.3 million recorded in 2024. The board attributes the decline mainly to weaker revenue caused by delayed implementation of certain government policies, changes or delays in customer procurement tied to enhanced product specifications and testing, and the discontinuation of some product lines.

The company also cites higher general and administrative expenses, including non-recurring listing costs linked to its June 2025 H-share listing in Hong Kong and increased staff costs from expanded headcount and payroll. Selling and marketing expenses have risen due to stepped-up e-commerce activities, while the potential financial impact of an ongoing civil complaint remains under assessment and may yet affect the final audited results, prompting management to urge investors to exercise caution until full annual figures are released.

More about Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. Class H

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong via H shares. The group operates in the technology and electronics sector, supplying specialized products to both government-related and commercial customers, with a growing focus on e-commerce channels for distribution and sales.
